preview_text:
The model favors Japan with a 69% win probability, substantially ahead of Tunisia's 9% chance. This gap reflects a decisive asymmetry in the forecast, though the 22% draw probability suggests the match carries meaningful uncertainty despite the clear favorite.

The outcome distribution shows Japan as the likely winner, but the draw sits at nearly one-quarter of all outcomes, indicating competitive conditions are plausible. Tunisia's path to victory is narrow in the model's view, yet the combined non-Japan probability (31%) is not negligible, meaning upsets or stalemates remain within the realm of statistical possibility.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge materially across all three outcomes. The market assigns Tunisia 20.6% (12 percentage points higher than the model), draw 28.3% (6pp higher), and Japan 51.1% (18pp lower). The gap suggests the books view this as a more open contest. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct; this divergence warrants attention from users calibrating their own views.
